speaker: Narrator
text: All Christina needed to clear her mind was to fill her stomach with some food. She walked into the kitchen and made her first realization.

speaker: Christina
text: Shit, the dishes.

speaker: Narrator
text: She hadn't done the dishes in a few days. But she could salvage something in a moment. Then another thought came.

speaker: Christina
text: I left something in the microwave for how long?

speaker: Christina
text: Oh god...

speaker: Christina
text: At least a week!

speaker: Christina
text: Do I even have any fucking food?

speaker: Narrator
text:
She opens up the refrigerator. Some of the items here have been there since Uncle Magnus left several years ago and are therefore inedible. 
	
	Not including those, there was a week-old coffee with a ring of scum on top, and a bagel and lox sandwich. She sniffed the bagel and found it to be spoiled
	
	After searching a bit more, she discovers a half-eaten kebab that is now desiccated to resemble a lump of coal resting on a bed of rice topped with delectable frosting of mold. 
	
	She had gotten some fresh fruits from a sale at the university, but she couldn't remember how long ago that was.

speaker: Narrator
text:
She opens the lower drawer and sees the bag she left the produce in, still disorganized. 
	
	Some of the items have managed to survive in a way, though they have managed to live by floating about the swamp of mush.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Ok, I'm wrapping up for dinner, but I want your takes on this chat...

speaker: Pasaya
text: Where's the best place to get a burger in your country?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Tell me!

speaker: Christina
text: There's this overpriced place that's a quick walk away from my place I think?

speaker: Christina
text: I don't like hamburgers, and there's the-

speaker: Pasaya
text: Please, I'm starving! Your queen is wasting away!

speaker: Christina
text: But, I'm not dressed, my phone isn't charged, I ran out of deodorant, and my hair is all messed up!

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'll die. Do you want your queen, your Pasaya to die?

speaker: Christina
text: No!

speaker: Pasaya
text: Then do as I say.

speaker: Christina
text: Fine.

speaker: Christina
text: I've got a Max around me, but their burgers can get kinda get soggy.

speaker: Pasaya
text: All you guys in the hive are making great choices tonight.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I bet that's a wonderful place to eat. Why don't you go there?
